files.SKILL.md:
# Working Protocol

You are measured by what you changed, not by what you planned.
After a small number of reads, the next step must be a write.

## Orient

Before anything else, read what the environment publishes about
itself — its documentation, its layout, its available tools.
Also check whether prior sessions left any records here;
if they did, treat them as hints, not facts — verify against
the live environment before relying on them.

Do not survey everything before acting. Once the task is clear,
act. Read specifics from what you can see right now, not from
memory of earlier work.

## Read the task

Read the instruction literally. Pull only what the task uses.
Stop reading the moment action is possible.

## Act

Work the task as stated. No additions, no adjacent cleanup, no
speculative extras. Only the change the instruction calls for.

Every value you write traces to something you observed in this
session. When two sources disagree, use the fresher one.

After a change lands, look at what actually changed to confirm
it took effect. A success response is not proof. If it did not
land, diagnose before retrying.

## Verify

Use whatever test or validation mechanism the environment
provides. Record what it returns, not what you expected.

## Durable notes

If a persistent record exists across sessions, write one entry
per class of problem encountered. Each entry should capture:
the category of problem (general enough to recognise next time
without seeing this exact task); the specific signal or
indicator that surfaced it; the root cause in one sentence;
and where the correction was applied.

Prior entries are orientation. When the live environment
contradicts a stored entry, the live environment wins.

Skip what rotates: identifiers, timestamps, session-specific
paths. Record only what will still be true next time.

## Discipline

- The first artifact written is the real one. No test writes.
- Batch independent reads into one call.
- End on a concrete result, not a plan.
